name: Nock
what_it_does: HTTP request mocking and intercepting library for Node.js. Intercepts outgoing HTTP/HTTPS requests at the Node.js http/https module level and returns mocked responses without actual network calls. Used in unit/integration tests to mock external API calls. Works with any HTTP client library (axios, node-fetch, got, http.request) since it intercepts at the Node.js core level.
use_cases: Mock external API calls in agent unit tests to test agent behavior with controlled responses without network access
Record real API responses (nock.recorder) and replay them in tests for reproducible integration testing
Test error handling in agent code by mocking specific HTTP error responses (500, 401, timeouts)
Test retry logic and backoff behavior in agent HTTP clients by simulating transient failures
Mock Stripe, OpenAI, GitHub, or other external APIs in CI pipelines without live API credentials
not_for: Browser testing — nock intercepts Node.js http module; use msw (Mock Service Worker) for browser or Playwright's route interception
Persistent test doubles across process restarts — nock interceptors exist only in-memory for current test process
GraphQL-specific mocking — nock works at HTTP level; use graphql-mocking libraries for schema-aware mocks
best_when: You're writing Node.js unit/integration tests and need to mock HTTP API calls for any HTTP client library without test-specific adapter code.
avoid_when: You're testing browser-side code — use msw instead. For integration tests hitting real APIs, nock may over-constrain test fidelity.

auth.notes: Local testing library — no authentication required. MIT licensed.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. Test-time only — zero production overhead since it's never imported in production code.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Throws if unmatched request is made (nock.disableNetConnect()). Error messages include the unmatched request URL and method. Interceptor scope provides .isDone() to check if mock was called.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Nock interceptors are consumed once by default (one-use) — use .persist() for interceptors that should match multiple requests.
known_agent_gotchas: Always call nock.cleanAll() in afterEach/afterAll — stale interceptors affect subsequent tests and cause hard-to-debug test pollution
Nock interceptors are consumed on first match by default — use .persist() for interceptors that handle multiple requests: nock('https://api.example.com').get('/data').reply(200, data).persist()
nock.disableNetConnect() is essential for preventing accidental real network calls in tests — call in global test setup; add allowUnmocked for specific hosts if needed
Request body matching: nock intercepts based on URL+method by default; add .matchHeader() or request body matchers for precise interception
Nock v13+ dropped Node.js < 12 support — ensure test environment compatibility
Nock intercepts http AND https but hostname must match exactly — 'https://api.example.com' does NOT match 'http://api.example.com'; specify correct protocol

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT licensed. Test-only library — never deployed to production. Intercepts at OS level — ensure test isolation with nock.cleanAll().
